name: System Architecture
description: System architecture overview including module dependencies, initialization flows, query/mutation execution patterns, and error handling hierarchy.

intent.what: Provides the structural blueprint of the Supabase backend template, showing how all modules interact and depend on each other.
intent.why: Enables AI agents to understand the system layout, identify correct import paths, trace execution flows, and locate code for any capability.

inputs.name: none
inputs.description: This skill is reference-only; no runtime inputs required.

outputs.description: Architectural knowledge including module tree, dependency graph, initialization sequence, and error class hierarchy.

steps: Read this skill to understand system structure before writing or modifying code
Use module dependency graph to determine correct import paths
Follow client initialization flow when setting up Supabase connection
Reference query/mutation execution flows when debugging or extending operations
Use error handling hierarchy to select appropriate error class for new operations

examples.description: Trace a query execution through the system layers

logic.summary: The system is organized in three layers: Application Layer (QueryEngine, Auth Middleware, Rate Limiter), Core Libraries (Auth, Database, Storage, Cache, Utils), and Supabase Client. All core libraries depend on the Supabase client at the base. Error handling flows upward from the client through all layers.

flow.detail: createSupabaseClient(config) or initializeSupabase() creates singleton instance
Checks VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from process.env or import.meta.env
Method called → validation → cache key derivation → cache check → query building → retry/timeout execution → caching → return
Method called → validation → query execution → cache invalidation → return
handleSupabaseError() catches raw errors and maps to typed SupabaseError subclasses

rules: Never bypass the client singleton pattern; always use getSupabaseClient()
All modules must import from lib/index.ts barrel exports, not internal paths
Cache must be invalidated after every mutation operation
Error handling must use typed error classes, not raw Error objects
Query execution must include retry and timeout handling for production use
anti_patterns: Do not create multiple Supabase client instances
Do not import from internal module paths directly in application code
Do not skip cache invalidation after mutations
Do not throw raw Error objects; always use typed SupabaseError subclasses
Do not bypass the QueryEngine for cached operations

functions.name: createSupabaseClient
functions.description: Creates and initializes the Supabase client singleton

functions.name: initializeSupabase
functions.description: Auto-initializes from environment or explicit config

functions.name: getSupabaseClient
functions.description: Retrieves singleton instance, auto-initializes if needed

functions.name: handleSupabaseError
functions.description: Normalizes any error into typed SupabaseError subclass

when_to_use: When first analyzing the codebase structure
When determining correct import paths for new code
When tracing execution flow for debugging
When extending the system with new modules
when_not_to_use: When writing domain-specific business logic (use database/auth skills instead)
When performing CRUD operations (use database skill instead)
When handling authentication (use auth skill instead)
reasoning_steps: Identify which layer the operation belongs to (application, core, client)
Trace the dependency chain to find required imports
Follow the execution flow (query or mutation) to understand behavior
Map error types to the appropriate SupabaseError subclass
Verify cache invalidation is handled for mutation operations
